text.tspan: Adaptive fractional dynamics in the visual cortex

tspan.tspan: School of Physics, The University of Sydney

text.tspan: 1 | What is the working regime of the visual cortex?
2 | How do we capture anomalous dynamics?
Complex cortical dynamics are not consistent with the normal diffusion
of the classical mean field [6], instead exhibiting anomalous diffusion.
Mean-field theory of classical E-I networks captures the fluctuation-
driven regime, but struggles to explain complex dynamics.
We generalize classical mean-field theory using fractional calculus:
• Space-fractional: power-law step size dist. drives superdiffusion
• Time-fractional: power-law memory kernel adds long-range memory
We use the bi-fractional model as an empirical mean-field input current,
allowing for tunable superdiffusion and long-range memory. We find:
• Long-range memory ➔ power-law Fano factor, tempered by oscillations
The competition between space-fractional superdiffusion and
time-fractional subdiffusion may help cortical circuits navigate the
